paper_title: Morphological and Morphometric Analysis of Tayassuidae Specimens

methods.description: Process for collecting and identifying specimens of Sylvochoerus woodburnei, Waldochoerus bassleri, and Surameryx acrensis for anatomical and taxonomic analysis.

steps.description: The specimens studied in this contribution correspond to those assigned to Sylvochoerus woodburnei and Waldochoerus bassleri (Tayassuidae) by Frailey and Campbell (2012) and Surameryx acrensis (Dromomerycinae cervoid) by Prothero et al. (2014).
steps.searchable_summary: collect specimens of Sylvochoerus woodburnei, Waldochoerus bassleri, and Surameryx acrensis

steps.description: Most tayassuid specimens are from the Harvey Bassler Collection of Peruvian Fossils, now housed at the U.S. National Museum of Natural History.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve tayassuid specimens from Harvey Bassler Collection

steps.description: A few specimens of peccaries are housed at the American Museum of Natural History, Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County, and University of California Museum of Paleontology.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve peccary specimens from various museums

methods.description: Method for comparing anatomical features and taxonomic classification of collected specimens with existing literature and collections.

steps.description: For anatomical comparisons, we followed the unpublished Ph.D. dissertations of two of the authors of the present contribution.
steps.searchable_summary: compare anatomical features using Ph.D. dissertations

steps.description: For nomenclatural assignments of collared and white-lipped peccaries, we followed Acosta et al. (2020).
steps.searchable_summary: assign nomenclature using Acosta et al. (2020)

steps.description: The holotype and sole specimen of Surameryx acrensis was compared to species of Palaeomerycidae and fossil and extant cervid species.
steps.searchable_summary: compare Surameryx acrensis to Palaeomerycidae and cervid species
